description: Reference parameters for the small intestine gut-lumen compartment relevant to ABCG2-mediated urate transport
volume_source: Commonly cited reference value for fasted small intestinal fluid volume; Johnson 1997 GI physiology; 250 mL is the conservative (high) estimate — lower volume estimates (100-150 mL proximal SI) would increase effective concentration
ph_source: Proximal small intestine pH range; ABCG2 is expressed throughout SI with peak in duodenum/jejunum
transit_note: Small intestine transit ~2-4h; solubility-limited drugs reach peak concentration in proximal SI before dilution/transit
abcg2_expression_note: ABCG2 highest in duodenum and proximal jejunum (apical surface). The 250 mL whole-SI estimate over-dilutes relative to the proximal compartment where ABCG2 density is highest — effective inhibition concentrations may be higher than model outputs suggest.
modeling_approach: Effective dissolved concentration = min(dose-limited concentration, solubility cap). Risk ratio = effective concentration / IC50. Hill equation with n=1: fractional inhibition = ratio / (ratio + 1).
